title: History compression â€” add Claude Haiku fallback when Groq is rate-limited (mirrors T-071 pattern)
current_state: When self.messages reaches >=30 entries (pi_agent.py:818), _truncate_messages_safely calls compress_messages_with_groq which uses Groq llama-3.3-70b to summarize the oldest half into a context block (cheap, zero Claude cost). If Groq is rate-limited at that moment (e.g. TPD exhausted, observed daily per S-053 on free tier), the call fails and the code falls back to truncate_messages_safely which hard-drops messages and loses context. Mid-conversation context loss is exactly the failure mode T-071 fixed for memory distillation â€” the same pattern is missing here.
structural_problem: Two compression-pipeline paths in the codebase: memory distillation (memory/pipeline.py, T-071 hardened with Groq â†’ Haiku â†’ regex fallback) and history compression (agent/truncation.py, only Groq with hard-truncate fallback). The inconsistency is the bug â€” when one pipeline fails the same way the other was hardened against, we already know what the fix looks like but haven't applied it.
target_state: compress_messages_with_groq becomes compress_messages_with_fallback (or keep name and add internal fallback). Provider chain: Groq llama-3.3-70b â†’ Claude Haiku 4.5 â†’ hard truncation (last resort, current behavior). The Haiku call uses the existing self.claude client. Same threshold (30) and keep_recent (12) parameters.
files_affected: agent/truncation.py (compress_messages_with_groq gains anthropic_client param + Haiku fallback branch)
pi_agent.py (line ~819: pass self.claude into the compress call)
success_criteria: agent/truncation.py compress_* function tries Groq first; on RateLimitError or APIStatusError falls back to Claude Haiku 4.5
On both LLMs failing: degrades to truncate_messages_safely (current hard-truncation behavior)
Failure path logged: 'history compression: Groq failed (X), trying Haiku' / 'history compression: both LLMs failed, hard-truncating'
Test: mock Groq to raise RateLimitError â†’ assert Haiku is called â†’ assert compressed message returned
Test: mock both â†’ assert hard truncation is the outcome
risk_notes: Pure additive. Worst case (both LLMs down) reverts to current behavior â€” strictly better than today.
migration_plan: 1. Add anthropic_client parameter to compress_messages_with_groq signature (default None for backward compat)
2. Wrap existing Groq call in try/except RateLimitError, APIStatusError, generic Exception
3. On failure: if anthropic_client provided, call claude-haiku-4-5 with same prompt and max_tokens; log the failover
4. On Haiku also failing: log and fall through to truncate_messages_safely as before
5. Update pi_agent.py:_truncate_messages_safely to pass self.claude into the call
6. Add tests under tests/test_compression_fallback.py covering both fallback transitions
7. Append SOLUTIONS.jsonl referencing T-071 as the pattern source
resolution: compress_messages_with_groq in agent/truncation.py gains anthropic_client parameter + Haiku 4.5 fallback branch. Provider chain: Groq llama-3.3-70b -> Claude Haiku 4.5 -> hard truncation. pi_agent.py passes self.claude into the call. 6 tests added in testing/test_compression_fallback.py (all pass). verify.py PASS.
